revision.comment: removed [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]; added [[Category:Completion tier 5]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[bakufu]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[bakufu]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

[[Category:qq]][[en:Tonegawa Zushi]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tonegawa Zushi}}

'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[bakufu]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qq" to "11"
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[bakufu]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.

'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[bakufu]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[bakufu]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.

'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]][[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[bakufu]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.

revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]][[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[bakufu]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Bump-01-18]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]][[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[bakufu]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.

'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[bakufu]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 6]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[bakufu]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Tier 6]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]][[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[bakufu]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

revision.text:
『'''利根川図志'''』（とねがわずし）は、[[江戸時代]]末期に[[赤松宗旦]]（あかまつそうたん）が著した[[利根川]]中・下流域の[[地誌]]である。 == 解題 == 執筆時期については、序文に[[江戸時代]]末期の[[安政]]2年（1855年）と記されている赤松 宗旦『利根川図志』（柳田國男 校訂、岩波文庫）、1938年、28-33頁（自序、凡例）。また、『利根川図志調帳』には、「安政4年（1857年）10月に『利根川図志』が完成し、取材協力者に送った」とあるので、この頃には製本が完了したことが分かる川名 登　『評伝　赤松宗旦－『利根川図志』が出来るまで』　彩流社、2010年、157-161頁（『利根川図志』の完成）。安政5年（1858年）4月、幕府から正式に出版が許可され、頒布されるようになった川名 登　『評伝　赤松宗旦－『利根川図志』が出来るまで』　彩流社、2010年、184-197頁（諸国売弘めの許可）。 執筆者の[[赤松宗旦]]（赤松義知）は、[[下総国]][[相馬郡 (下総国)|相馬郡]][[布川町|布川村]]（現在の[[茨城県]][[北相馬郡]][[利根町]]布川）の医師。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（1806年）生まれ。父の恵も宗旦と称しており、義知は二代目であった川名 登　『評伝　赤松宗旦－『利根川図志』が出来るまで』　彩流社、2010年、30-39頁（『利根川図志』の著者－宗旦義知）。 紹介されている地域は[[利根川]]中・下流域。現在の[[渡良瀬川]]合流地点から[[太平洋]]河口まで、すなわち、[[茨城県]][[古河市]]から[[千葉県]][[銚子市]]までの広い流域を対象とし、各地の名所・旧跡・名産品・風土・風習などを、多数の挿絵を交えて紹介している赤松宗旦『利根川図志』（柳田國男校訂・解題、岩波文庫、初版1938年）、3-14頁。 挿絵を描いた絵師も多彩で、宗旦自身が残した『雑話』には、葛飾北斎巻一の凡例にも「巻中の画図名印無きは葛飾北斎なり」とあるが、[[葛飾北斎]]は嘉永2年（1849年）に90歳で没している。そこでこれは[[葛飾北斎 (2代目)|二代目北斎]]だろうといわれている（川名登『評伝　赤松宗旦－「利根川図志」が出来るまで』208頁）。また岩波文庫版『[{{NDLDC|1225314}} 利根川図志]』の解題には、「土地の人の言つて居るのは、北斎は一時師匠の許をしくぢつて、爰へ来て匿れて居たことがある。其間に描いたものだから落款を入れていない」という真偽不明の話を伝えており、「或はその又弟子の一人などが、中に立つて何か策略したことが、斯ういふ風に誤り伝へられたのであらう」と述べている。、[[葛飾為斎]]、[[山形素真]]、[[湖城喜一]]、[[歌川貞秀|玉蘭斎貞秀]]、[[歌川広重|一立斎広重]]の名が見える川名 登　『評伝　赤松宗旦－『利根川図志』が出来るまで』　彩流社、2010年、207-223頁（『利根川図志』に画いた絵師たち）。 続編については凡例に、「而して上利根川の方、亦継で筆を起こさむとす、その考察においては、亦上武諸哲の教を期つ」とあり、宗旦は上流域を対象とした続巻の構想を持っていたことが分かる。実際に、『利根川図志後篇　草稿巻一』、および、「利根川水源并支流名称」・「利根川御用御船印」・「木下シ旅人船御定」の記載がある史料が残されている。しかし執筆途中の[[文久]]2年（1862年）、宗旦は57歳で死去し、続編は完成しなかった川名 登　『評伝　赤松宗旦－『利根川図志』が出来るまで』　彩流社、2010年、198-202頁（「続編」の執筆と死）。 == 構成 == 本書の構成は下記の通り赤松宗旦『利根川図志』（柳田国男 校訂、岩波文庫）、1938年。 {| class="wikitable" |- ! 巻数 !! 項目名 !! 分類 !! 現在の市町村 !! 大字等の位置情報 !! 挿絵 |- |rowspan="5"|巻一 | 序・自序・凡例 | | | | [[利根川]]全図（1～12）（[[絵地図]]）
上利根川余五橋図（[[風景画]]） |- | 総論 | | | | |- | 運輸 | | | | 鯛魚（[[博物画|動物画]]） |- | 天候 | | | | 布川魚市之光景（[[鳥瞰図]]） |- | 物産 | | | | 鮭魚（動物画）
サカベツタウ考（動物画・[[博物画|植物画]]）

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[利根川図志]] to [[Tonegawa Zushi]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
『'''利根川図志'''』（とねがわずし）は、[[江戸時代]]末期に[[赤松宗旦]]（あかまつそうたん）が著した[[利根川]]中・下流域の[[地誌]]である。 == 解題 == 執筆時期については、序文に[[江戸時代]]末期の[[安政]]2年（1855年）と記されている赤松 宗旦『利根川図志』（柳田國男 校訂、岩波文庫）、1938年、28-33頁（自序、凡例）。また、『利根川図志調帳』には、「安政4年（1857年）10月に『利根川図志』が完成し、取材協力者に送った」とあるので、この頃には製本が完了したことが分かる川名 登　『評伝　赤松宗旦－『利根川図志』が出来るまで』　彩流社、2010年、157-161頁（『利根川図志』の完成）。安政5年（1858年）4月、幕府から正式に出版が許可され、頒布されるようになった川名 登　『評伝　赤松宗旦－『利根川図志』が出来るまで』　彩流社、2010年、184-197頁（諸国売弘めの許可）。 執筆者の[[赤松宗旦]]（赤松義知）は、[[下総国]][[相馬郡 (下総国)|相馬郡]][[布川町|布川村]]（現在の[[茨城県]][[北相馬郡]][[利根町]]布川）の医師。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]3年（1806年）生まれ。父の恵も宗旦と称しており、義知は二代目であった川名 登　『評伝　赤松宗旦－『利根川図志』が出来るまで』　彩流社、2010年、30-39頁（『利根川図志』の著者－宗旦義知）。 紹介されている地域は[[利根川]]中・下流域。現在の[[渡良瀬川]]合流地点から[[太平洋]]河口まで、すなわち、[[茨城県]][[古河市]]から[[千葉県]][[銚子市]]までの広い流域を対象とし、各地の名所・旧跡・名産品・風土・風習などを、多数の挿絵を交えて紹介している赤松宗旦『利根川図志』（柳田國男校訂・解題、岩波文庫、初版1938年）、3-14頁。 挿絵を描いた絵師も多彩で、宗旦自身が残した『雑話』には、葛飾北斎巻一の凡例にも「巻中の画図名印無きは葛飾北斎なり」とあるが、[[葛飾北斎]]は嘉永2年（1849年）に90歳で没している。そこでこれは[[葛飾北斎 (2代目)|二代目北斎]]だろうといわれている（川名登『評伝　赤松宗旦－「利根川図志」が出来るまで』208頁）。また岩波文庫版『[{{NDLDC|1225314}} 利根川図志]』の解題には、「土地の人の言つて居るのは、北斎は一時師匠の許をしくぢつて、爰へ来て匿れて居たことがある。其間に描いたものだから落款を入れていない」という真偽不明の話を伝えており、「或はその又弟子の一人などが、中に立つて何か策略したことが、斯ういふ風に誤り伝へられたのであらう」と述べている。、[[葛飾為斎]]、[[山形素真]]、[[湖城喜一]]、[[歌川貞秀|玉蘭斎貞秀]]、[[歌川広重|一立斎広重]]の名が見える川名 登　『評伝　赤松宗旦－『利根川図志』が出来るまで』　彩流社、2010年、207-223頁（『利根川図志』に画いた絵師たち）。 続編については凡例に、「而して上利根川の方、亦継で筆を起こさむとす、その考察においては、亦上武諸哲の教を期つ」とあり、宗旦は上流域を対象とした続巻の構想を持っていたことが分かる。実際に、『利根川図志後篇　草稿巻一』、および、「利根川水源并支流名称」・「利根川御用御船印」・「木下シ旅人船御定」の記載がある史料が残されている。しかし執筆途中の[[文久]]2年（1862年）、宗旦は57歳で死去し、続編は完成しなかった川名 登　『評伝　赤松宗旦－『利根川図志』が出来るまで』　彩流社、2010年、198-202頁（「続編」の執筆と死）。 == 構成 == 本書の構成は下記の通り赤松宗旦『利根川図志』（柳田国男 校訂、岩波文庫）、1938年。 {| class="wikitable" |- ! 巻数 !! 項目名 !! 分類 !! 現在の市町村 !! 大字等の位置情報 !! 挿絵 |- |rowspan="5"|巻一 | 序・自序・凡例 | | | | [[利根川]]全図（1～12）（[[絵地図]]）

revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Tonegawa Zushi}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{DEFAULTSORT\:[^]]+}}" to ""
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:取手市の歴史
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}

}}

== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:香取市の歴史
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}

}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kantō region history
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[bakufu]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Hitachi Province]]
}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Hitachi Province]]
}}
