revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Hitachi Province]]
}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:銚子の Municipal History]] into [[Category:Municipal History of Chōshi]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Hitachi Province]]
}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[:en:Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Hitachi Province]]
}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:河内の Municipal History]] into [[Category:Municipal History of Kawachi]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[:en:Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Hitachi Province]]
}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[:en:Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Hitachi Province]]
}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:江戸時代の地誌]] into [[Category:Regional geography of the Edo period]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[:en:Tonegawa|Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[:en:Tone, Ibaraki|Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[:en:Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Hitachi Province]]
}}

== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:利根川図志]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17229342]] Wikidata name: Tonegawa Zushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[:en:Tonegawa|Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[:en:Tone, Ibaraki|Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[:en:Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[:en:Tone, Ibaraki|Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[:en:Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:利根川図志]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17229342]] Wikidata name: Tonegawa Zushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[:en:Tonegawa|Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[:en:Tone, Ibaraki|Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[:en:Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category: of *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>. The [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Hitachi Province]]
}}

Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[:en:Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:利根川図志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:利根川図志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:利根川図志]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17229342]] Wikidata name: Tonegawa Zushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[:en:Tonegawa|Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[:en:Tone, Ibaraki|Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[:en:Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:利根川図志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:利根川図志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:利根川図志]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17229342]] Wikidata name: Tonegawa Zushi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the [[:en:Tonegawa|Tonegawa]] River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].
== Overview == The text was written in [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[:en:Tone, Ibaraki|Tone, Ibaraki]])<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day [[:en:Koga, Ibaraki|Koga]] to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Overview == The text was written in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}} (present-day {{ill|Tone, Ibaraki|en|Tone, Ibaraki|WD=Q1203324|lt=Tone, Ibaraki}})<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the {{ill|Watarase River|en|Watarase River|WD=Q3566607|lt=Watarase River}} to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day {{ill|Koga, Ibaraki|en|Koga, Ibaraki|WD=Q836836|lt=Koga}} to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:利根川図志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:利根川図志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:利根川図志]]
'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the {{ill|Tonegawa|en|Tonegawa|WD=Q7820870|lt=Tonegawa}} River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}.
== Overview == The text was written in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}} (present-day {{ill|Tone, Ibaraki|en|Tone, Ibaraki|WD=Q1203324|lt=Tone, Ibaraki}})<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the {{ill|Watarase River|en|Watarase River|WD=Q3566607|lt=Watarase River}} to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day {{ill|Koga, Ibaraki|en|Koga, Ibaraki|WD=Q836836|lt=Koga}} to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
{{translated page|ja|利根川図志|version=100426281|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Tonegawa Zushi''' (利根川図志) is a geographical and cultural record of the mid- and lower reaches of the {{ill|Tonegawa|en|Tonegawa|WD=Q7820870|lt=Tonegawa|1=Tonegawa}} River, authored by [[Akamatsu Sōtan]] during the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}.
== Overview == The text was written in {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 2 (1855), as stated in the preface<ref name="jo">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938, pp. 28–33 (Preface and Notes)</ref>. According to *Tonegawa Zushi Chōchō*, the book was completed in October of Ansei 4 (1857) and distributed to contributors who assisted with the research<ref name="kawana157">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 157–161 (Completion tier 6]] officially approved its publication in Ansei 5 (1858)<ref name="kawana184">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 184–197 (Distribution Authorization)</ref>.
Akamatsu Sōtan (also known as Akamatsu Yoshitomo), a physician from [[Fukawa Village]] in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}} (present-day {{ill|Tone, Ibaraki|en|Tone, Ibaraki|WD=Q1203324|lt=Tone, Ibaraki|1=Tone, Ibaraki}})<ref name="kawana30">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 30–39 (Author Overview)</ref>, authored the work. Born in Bunka 3 (1806), Sōtan was the second to bear this name in his family, following his father.
The work focuses on the mid- and lower reaches of the Tonegawa River, from its confluence with the {{ill|Watarase River|en|Watarase River|WD=Q3566607|lt=Watarase River|1=Watarase River}} to its Pacific Ocean estuary, covering modern-day {{ill|Koga, Ibaraki|en|Koga, Ibaraki|WD=Q836836|lt=Koga|1=Koga, Ibaraki}} to [[:en:Chōshi, Chiba|Chōshi, Chiba]]. It documents regional landmarks, historical sites, local products, customs, and geography, supplemented by numerous illustrations<ref name="kaidai">Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), pp. 3–14 (Introduction)</ref>.
Contributing artists include notable figures such as [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]], [[Katsushika Isai]], [[Yamagata Sosin]], [[Kojo Kiichi]], [[Gyokuransei Sadahide]], and [[:en:Utagawa Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]<ref name="kawana207">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 207–223 (Artists in *Tonegawa Zushi*)</ref>.
Sōtan intended to write a sequel focusing on the upper reaches of the Tonegawa River. Drafts of *Tonegawa Zushi Kōhen* and related notes remain, but Sōtan’s death in {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 2 (1862) at the age of 57 left the sequel unfinished<ref name="kawana198">Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010, pp. 198–202 (Work on Sequel and Death)</ref>.
== Structure == The text is organized into six volumes, covering topics such as transportation, weather, regional products, and significant locations, accompanied by a range of illustrations, including maps, sketches, and bird’s-eye views.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == - Akamatsu Sōtan, *Tonegawa Zushi* (edited by Yanagita Kunio, Iwanami Bunko), 1938; reprint 1987. - Kawana Noboru, *Hiden: Akamatsu Sōtan – Tonegawa Zushi no Dekiru Made*, Sairyūsha, 2010. - [https://www.lib.pref.saitama.jp/stplib_doc/data/d_conts/kicho/syosai/032.html Saitama Prefectural Library Digital Archive – *Tonegawa Zushi*]
